articles.title: Vinit Mobile IPO opens today; GMP, subscription status, price band, other key details of SME IPO
articles.summary: Vinit Mobile IPO price band is set at ₹150 to ₹158 per share, and the IPO lot size is 800 shares. The company aims to raise ₹34.13 crore from the book-building issue, which is entirely a fresh issue of 21.60 lakh equity shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Teja Engineering Industries IPO opens today. GMP, subscription status, other details of NSE SME IPO in 10 points
articles.summary: Teja Engineering Industries IPO: According to market observers, shares of the company are available at par in the grey market today
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Healthcare stock Park Medi World hits record high despite weak trends on Dalal Street
articles.summary: Healthcare stock Park Medi World surged as much as 4.45% to 303.70 apiece in Monday's trading session despite weak market sentiments on Dalal Street.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger renewable energy stock Suzlon Energy share price gains on 105 MW order win; do you own?
articles.summary: Following the launch of the S175 wind turbine, Sunsure Energy has placed a 105 MW order with Suzlon, showcasing strong market confidence. The collaboration supports growth in renewable energy and aims to meet India's industrial energy needs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty IT plunges over 2%; Infosys, LTIMindtree, TCS among top laggards
articles.summary: On June 30, the Nifty IT index dropped over 2% due to global tech weaknesses and demand concerns. Key players like LTIMindtree, Infosys, and TCS led the decline, with analysts highlighting a crucial support level that could define the index's trajectory.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Japanese yen slides to 40-year low against the US dollar
articles.summary: The Japanese currency is on track to decline 2% in the second quarter, marking its fourth consecutive quarterly loss. This is its longest losing streak since 2022, when it fell for seven straight quarters, according to Reuters.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO GMP signals strong listing on July 1; check allotment status, subscription and other details
articles.summary: The Advit Jewels IPO received significant investor interest, being oversubscribed 212.63 times. The issue's price band is set at ₹130-138 per share. The company will debut on stock exchanges on July 1, with a grey market premium indicating a possible listing price of ₹184 per share.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oyo IPO: Parent Prism files updated DRHP for ₹6,650 crore public issue
articles.summary: According to the Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP) filed on Tuesday, the IPO will consist of a fresh issue of equity shares worth up to ₹6,650 crore, marking the company's latest bid to enter the public markets.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Knack Packaging IPO opens tomorrow. GMP, date, size to review in 10 points
articles.summary: Knack Packaging IPO GMP today: According to market observers, shares of the company are available at a premium of ₹5 in the grey market today
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bitcoin price falls below $60,000 again: Experts reveal key levels to watch for the cryptocurrency - What lies ahead?
articles.summary: Bitcoin struggles near $58,000 as bearish sentiment prevails. The market looks towards US economic reports and expected interest rate hikes, causing subdued trading and cautious investor behavior across digital assets.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Aastha Spintex IPO Day 2: Issue booked 1.04x so far. Check GMP, subscription, review. Apply or skip?
articles.summary: The Aastha Spintex IPO is priced between ₹125-136 and opens for subscription until 1 July. It has allocated 20% for QIBs, 40% for NIIs, and 40% for retail investors, with allotments expected on 2 July and listing on 6 July.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Gold rate drops near ₹1,40,500 per 10 grams on MCX; silver declines below ₹2,21,000 per kg; what should investors do?
articles.summary: MCX gold August futures were 1.28% down at ₹1,40,574 per 10 grams, while MCX silver September contracts were 1.04% down at ₹2,20,322 per kg around 9:10 am.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Rupee opens marginally weaker at 94.57 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened weaker at 94.57 against the US dollar, hindered by high demand for dollars despite a recovery from its record low. The rupee remains range-bound as global tensions and foreign investor caution affect the currency and market sentiment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: SIS announces ₹120 crore share buyback, fifth since listing; check details
articles.summary: SIS Ltd has proposed a ₹120 crore share buyback, its fifth since 2017. The maximum buyback price is set at ₹478.50 per share. This follows an 8% stock rise in a month and highlights the company's commitment to returning around ₹720 crore to shareholders through various initiatives.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy for short term: Trent, Torrent Pharma among 6 stocks experts recommend for next 1-2 weeks
articles.summary: Stocks to buy for the short term: Experts highlight six stocks poised for short-term gains. From Trent, Lloyds Metals to Torrent Pharma, these companies showcase strong momentum backed by technical indicators.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CSM Technologies IPO allotment date likely today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status online
articles.summary: CSM Technologies IPO allotment status is expected to be finalised today, 30 June 2026. CSM Technologies IPO allotment status online check can be done through the websites of BSE and NSE, and also on the official portal of the IPO registrar. Kfin Technologies is the CSM Technologies IPO registrar.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints muted start; US-Iran talks to global markets - 7 stocks to buy or sell
articles.summary: Indian stock market indices, Sensex and Nifty 50, are expected to open positively on Tuesday amid rising global markets, despite geopolitical tensions due to US-Iran conflicts and profit booking. Experts suggest cautious trading influenced by upcoming negotiations and regional developments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 30 June
articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a mildly positive start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 23,994 level, a premium of nearly 21 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.

articles.title: Crude oil prices fall on possible US-Iran talks; Brent near $72 per barrel
articles.summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures for August delivery, set to expire on Tuesday, fell 1.03%, or 75 cents, to $72.40 per barrel.

articles.title: From Gift Nifty, Nasdaq, Nikkei rally to gold rate: 10 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
articles.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,011 level, a premium of nearly 38 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a mildly positive start for the Indian stock market indices.

articles.summary: Asian stocks today: Japan's Nikkei 225 remained volatile and traded largely flat and the Topix was down 0.65%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today - 30 June 2026

articles.title: Sebi panel weighs proposal to open physically settled commodity trades to FPIs
articles.summary: The proposal seeks to address a long-standing tax hurdle that has kept FPIs out of bullion and base metal derivatives, where contracts are compulsorily deliverable on expiry.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: How retail came to life in currency derivatives during war
articles.summary: As local unit plunges 6.5% participation as percentage of turnover almost doubles; brokers say such trades are hedges against currency volatility
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: SpaceX shares slide 32% from record high, but remain 13% above IPO price
articles.summary: SpaceX's shares have dropped 32% from their peak after a record-breaking IPO, erasing initial gains. Despite this, the stock remains above its IPO price. SpaceX is also set to join the Nasdaq-100 Index, which could boost demand from investment funds.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold and silver prices fall as Middle East tensions spark Fed rate hike fears
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices fell again on 29 June, driven by Middle East tensions and inflation concerns. Comex gold futures dropped to an intraday low of $4,012, while silver fell to $57.84, marking a significant downturn for both metals amid rate hike fears.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Comcast shares surge 17% as company announces NBCUniversal, Sky spin-off plan
articles.summary: Comcast shares rose 17% to $27.10 after announcing plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky, creating a standalone media company. This move aims to help both entities focus on their strategic priorities amid ongoing industry changes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Himadri Speciality Chemicals bets big on EV battery materials with 100 GWh manufacturing facility
articles.summary: Himadri Speciality Chemicals plans to establish India's largest battery materials manufacturing facility, expecting a revenue boost of ₹30,000 crore over five years. The 100 GWh facility will produce key components for EV batteries, alongside a 40,000 metric tonnes LFP cathode plant.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures jump as investors buy the tech dip; oil near 4-month low
articles.summary: US stock futures rose slightly on 29 June as investors returned to technology stocks after recent losses. The S&P 500 futures increased by 0.08%, while Nasdaq 100 futures gained 1.1%.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Expert view: See compelling setup for Indian stocks to outperform, says Bajaj Finserv Asset Management's head of equity
articles.summary: Expert view: Bajaj Finserv's head of equity emphasises why Indian stocks are set to outperform other emerging markets. With improving foreign investor sentiment and attractive valuations, now may be the time for disciplined, long-term investment in equities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The human cost of India’s new welfare architecture, explained in charts
articles.summary: India is shifting from rights-based welfare to new welfarism, prioritizing centralized cash transfers and tangible goods over public services. This transition strains state finances and risks eroding long-term human development.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The women behind India’s economy—and the price of invisible work
articles.summary: A recent Supreme Court judgment places economic value on the work done by homemakers. That value won’t make it into GDP math, but should find its way into policymaking.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Freight rates to stay elevated amid high demand for tankers, vessels
articles.summary: Global shipping freight rates are expected to stay high for the coming months due to increased demand from recovering trade flows and the peak shipping season, despite a slight easing in rates following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 5.1% in May under new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: Growth was led by a robust expansion in manufacturing and supported by moderate growth in mining and electricity, according to provisional data released by the statistics ministry on Monday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India regains top spot in EM tracker, but higher cost pressures continue to linger
articles.summary: Stronger exports and factory activity helped India reclaim the top spot in Mint's Emerging Markets Tracker. However, higher crude prices during the West Asia war have raised wholesale inflation and input costs, delaying relief for businesses despite the recent easing in oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: North-East floods raise power, logistics and inflation risks
articles.summary: The disruptions could have wider consequences for the rest of the country, where power demand remains high amid persistent heat and an uneven monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Modi spotlights shift to public transport, natural fertilizers amid Gulf crisis
articles.summary: Addressing the nation in his monthly radio broadcast ‘Mann Ki Baat’, Modi said that following his appeal to avoid foreign travel and cut gold purchases, many people have postponed overseas holidays and chosen not to buy new gold, with some instead recycling old gold to make new jewellery.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt eases Quality Control Order rules: Which industry segments are likely to get relief?
articles.summary: The government's new transition framework offers relief to manufacturers in sectors such as toys, personal protective equipment (PPE), air conditioners, footwear, furniture and select electrical appliances.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.summary: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Iranian oil may return, but Indian refiners eye concessions
articles.summary: As Indian refiners await clarity in terms of payment mechanism and long-term durability of the US-Iran peace talks, they may not rush for immediate procurement of Iranian crude and are taking a more long-term approach, to ensure supplies when the sanctions are permanently waived off
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Will water shortages derail India’s AI Data Centre boom?
articles.summary: India's ambition to become a leading AI data centre hub brings unprecedented investments, but it also raises crucial questions about sustainability.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Weak core output, Russian crude imports, AI edge
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RBI allows banks to give loans for FCNR deposits
articles.summary: On 5 June, RBI said it will absorb the foreign exchange hedging cost on fresh  CNR (B), deposits, creating an unusual opportunity for overseas Indians
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Moving decisively towards finalizing trade deal with India, says US Ambassador Gor
articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade chief to visit India for interim deal discussions
articles.summary: The visit comes amid efforts by India and the US to deepen economic ties and address market access issues through a comprehensive trade pact.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI governor asks banks to treat MSMEs as long-term partners
articles.summary: The RBI governor asked financial institutions to make greater use of India's digital public infrastructure
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
articles.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes
articles.label: NEUTRAL
